title: What the Gray-winged Fairy Said
content:
(Moon Poems for the Children/Fairy-tales for the Children)

         The moon's a gong, hung in the wild,
         Whose song the fays hold dear.
         Of course you do not hear it, child.
         It takes a FAIRY ear.

         The full moon is a splendid gong
         That beats as night grows still.
         It sounds above the evening song
         Of dove or whippoorwill.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Vachel Lindsay delivers a powerful performance in "What the Gray-winged Fairy Said"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
